perturbation.original_text: COOL TECH shall, in a professional manner, take all steps necessary to market and produce its Mobile Generation Program and its other services (collectively the "COOL TECH Services") for clients referred to COOL TECH by VET TECH. Any engagement to perform COOL TECH Services shall be on such terms and conditions as COOL TECH may approve at its sole discretion. COOL TECH will perform, schedule, staff and manage all COOL TECH Services or with COOL TECH/VET TECH approved team. Notwithstanding the foregoing, VET TECH may, at its election, bill the client directly for COOL TECH Services and under such circumstances COOL TECH shall bill VET TECH the pre-agreed amount for the engagement as adjusted by any client-approved change orders. For any Government Sales brought by VET TECH COOL TECH agrees to be held to the same billing and payment terms as is dictated under the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) guidelines and requirements; otherwise, COOL TECH will bill the client directly. VET TECH agrees to include reference to COOL TECH in each contract and proposal involving COOL TECH Services. COOL TECH's Mobile Generation ("MG"), and other proprietary information and associated products, copyrights, trademarks, trade names and logos developed by COOL TECH shall remain the property of COOL TECH and reference to COOL TECH's rights shall be made in all uses of such materials in at least 12 point type.
perturbation.changed_text: COOL TECH shall, in a professional manner, take all steps necessary to market and produce its Mobile Generation Program and its other services (collectively the "COOL TECH Solutions") for clients referred to COOL TECH by VET TECH. Any engagement to perform COOL TECH Solutions shall be on such terms and conditions as COOL TECH may approve at its sole discretion. COOL TECH will perform, schedule, staff and manage all COOL TECH Solutions or with COOL TECH/VET TECH approved team. Notwithstanding the foregoing, VET TECH may, at its election, bill the client directly for COOL TECH Solutions and under such circumstances COOL TECH shall bill VET TECH the pre-agreed amount for the engagement as adjusted by any client-approved change orders. For any Government Sales brought by VET TECH COOL TECH agrees to be held to the same billing and payment terms as is dictated under the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) guidelines and requirements; otherwise, COOL TECH will bill the client directly. VET TECH agrees to include reference to COOL TECH in each contract and proposal involving COOL TECH Solutions. COOL TECH's Mobile Generation ("MG"), and other proprietary information and associated products, copyrights, trademarks, trade names and logos developed by COOL TECH shall remain the property of COOL TECH and reference to COOL TECH's rights shall be made in all uses of such materials in at least 12 point type.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'COOL TECH Services' has been changed to 'COOL TECH Solutions.' This introduces ambiguity because the contract initially defines 'COOL TECH Services' but now uses 'COOL TECH Solutions' without defining it. The use of both terms interchangeably, without clarification, creates a contradiction. The document specifies COOL TECH Services but the meaning of COOL TECH Solutions remains uncertain. 'Services' and 'Solutions' are not synonyms, especially in the business context. The agreement text needs to define if they are referring to the same thing.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE I. SCOPE OF STRATEGIC ALLIANCE. A
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE I. SCOPE OF STRATEGIC ALLIANCE. A
perturbation.contradicted_text: COOL TECH shall, in a professional manner, take all steps necessary to market and produce its Mobile Generation Program and its other services (collectively the "COOL TECH Solutions") for clients referred to COOL TECH by VET TECH. Any engagement to perform COOL TECH Solutions shall be on such terms and conditions as COOL TECH may approve at its sole discretion. COOL TECH will perform, schedule, staff and manage all COOL TECH Solutions or with COOL TECH/VET TECH approved team. Notwithstanding the foregoing, VET TECH may, at its election, bill the client directly for COOL TECH Solutions and under such circumstances COOL TECH shall bill VET TECH the pre-agreed amount for the engagement as adjusted by any client-approved change orders. For any Government Sales brought by VET TECH COOL TECH agrees to be held to the same billing and payment terms as is dictated under the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) guidelines and requirements; otherwise, COOL TECH will bill the client directly. VET TECH agrees to include reference to COOL TECH in each contract and proposal involving COOL TECH Solutions. COOL TECH's Mobile Generation ("MG"), and other proprietary information and associated products, copyrights, trademarks, trade names and logos developed by COOL TECH shall remain the property of COOL TECH and reference to COOL TECH's rights shall be made in all uses of such materials in at least 12 point type.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'COOL TECH Services' to 'COOL TECH Solutions' creates a contradiction because the contract initially defines 'COOL TECH Services' but then uses 'COOL TECH Solutions' without providing a definition. This misalignment introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about which activities are covered. The Contradicted Text is the same text as the changed text, but the Change causes a contradiction to itself, because the text used to only mention 'COOL TECH Services' but now it only mentions 'COOL TECH Solutions.' This breaks compatibility because the document now refers to an undefined term. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, causing an In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall be earlier terminated (x) by mutual agreement of the parties, or (y) at any time upon sixty (60) days advance written notice to the other party. Time is of the essence in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall be earlier terminated (x) by mutual agreement of the parties, or (y) at any time upon ninety (90) days advance written notification to the other party. Time is of the essence in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changed "notice" to "notification" and changed the number of days from sixty(60) to ninety (90). While they are synonyms, using 'notification' after exclusively using 'notice' introduces ambiguity. One may infer that 'notification' has a specific legal meaning distinct from 'notice', which is undefined here. Additionally, the change in the numbers of days between termination of the agreement creates an In-Text Contradiction, where termination period is being modified without clarifying the difference.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the date first set forth above and, shall expire on the later of (i) five (5) years from the date hereof, or (ii) with respect to any projects identified in any contract for which VET TECH is billing the client directly, upon the completion of COOL TECH's Services and receipt of payment by COOL TECH from VET TECH for said services. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive one year periods unless either party gives written notice of termination to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the date of expiration. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall be earlier terminated (x) by mutual agreement of the parties, or (y) at any time upon ninety (90) days advance written notification to the other party. Time is of the essence in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'sixty (60) days advance written notice' to 'ninety (90) days advance written notification' directly contradicts the pre-existing clause in the contradicted text that states 'This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive one year periods unless either party gives written notice of termination to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the date of expiration.' The contradiction arises because the changed text introduces a ninety-day termination period, conflicting with the thirty-day notice period required for preventing automatic renewal. The introduction of 'notification' where 'notice' was exclusively used may also introduce ambiguity. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH, at its own expense, shall indemnify, defend and hold the other, its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, employees, and agents harmless from and against any and all third-party suits, actions, investigations and proceedings, and related costs and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) resulting solely and directly from the indemnifying party's negligence or willful misconduct. Neither VET TECH nor COOL TECH shall be required hereunder to defend, indemnify or hold harmless the other and/or its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, directors, employees and agents, or any of them, from any liability resulting from the negligence or wrongful acts of the party seeking indemnification or of any third- party. Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH agrees to give the other prompt written notice of any claim or other matter as to which it believes this indemnification provision is applicable. The indemnifying party shall have the right to defend against any such claim with counsel of its own choosing and to settle and/or compromise such claim as it deems appropriate. Each party further agrees to cooperate with the other in the defense of any such claim or other matter.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH, at its own expense, shall indemnify, defend and hold the other, its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, employees, and agents harmless from and against any and all third-party suits, actions, investigations and proceedings, and related costs and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) resulting solely and directly from the indemnifying party's negligence or intentional wrongdoing. Neither VET TECH nor COOL TECH shall be required hereunder to defend, indemnify or hold harmless the other and/or its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, directors, employees and agents, or any of them, from any liability resulting from the negligence or wrongful acts of the party seeking indemnification or of any third- party. Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH agrees to give the other prompt written notice of any claim or other matter as to which it believes this indemnification provision is applicable. The indemnifying party shall have the right to defend against any such claim with counsel of its own choosing and to settle and/or compromise such claim as it deems appropriate. Each party further agrees to cooperate with the other in the defense of any such claim or other matter.
perturbation.explanation: Changed "willful misconduct" to "intentional wrongdoing". This is a perturbation because the legal definition of 'willful misconduct' is distinct from 'intentional wrongdoing.' Willful misconduct often implies a higher degree of culpability or recklessness than simple intentional wrongdoing. Now there's confusion as to what needs to be demonstrated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH, at its own expense, shall indemnify, defend and hold the other, its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, employees, and agents harmless from and against any and all third-party suits, actions, investigations and proceedings, and related costs and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) resulting solely and directly from the indemnifying party's negligence or intentional wrongdoing. Neither VET TECH nor COOL TECH shall be required hereunder to defend, indemnify or hold harmless the other and/or its partners, shareholders, directors, officers, directors, employees and agents, or any of them, from any liability resulting from the negligence or wrongful acts of the party seeking indemnification or of any third- party. Each of VET TECH and COOL TECH agrees to give the other prompt written notice of any claim or other matter as to which it believes this indemnification provision is applicable. The indemnifying party shall have the right to defend against any such claim with counsel of its own choosing and to settle and/or compromise such claim as it deems appropriate. Each party further agrees to cooperate with the other in the defense of any such claim or other matter.
perturbation.justification: While the explanation notes a difference between 'willful misconduct' and 'intentional wrongdoing,' the changed text does not create a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. Both terms are used within the same clause, suggesting an attempt to clarify rather than introduce conflicting obligations. The potential difference in legal interpretation doesn't create a clear, in-text contradiction that makes enforcement impossible or creates a logical inconsistency within the document itself.
